items.title: Is Rigetti Computing Stock a Buy in 2025?
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Rigetti Computing, a quantum computer maker, has seen its stock surge 1,860% in the past six months. However, the company's quarterly revenue remains modest at $2.4 million, and it faces significant risks, including competition from tech giants and the long timeline for practical quantum computing applications.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article presents a balanced view of Rigetti Computing's potential and challenges. While the company has made technological advancements and has a clear roadmap, it faces significant competition and the timeline for practical quantum computing applications is still uncertain, leading to a neutral sentiment.

items.title: 1 Top AI Agent Stock to Buy Right Now
items.author: Timothy Green
items.description: IBM's watsonx platform and consulting arm provide enterprises with a full suite of AI tools and services, positioning the company well to capitalize on the rise of AI agents - narrow-focused AI systems that can autonomously handle related tasks. This combination of software and services gives IBM a unique advantage in the enterprise AI market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights IBM's strong position in the enterprise AI market, particularly with its watsonx platform and consulting services. This combination of software and services is seen as a unique advantage that puts IBM in a great position to ride the AI agent wave and enable enterprises to deploy AI effectively.
The article mentions that IBM's watsonx platform can run on Amazon's AWS, but this is not the main focus of the article, which is on IBM's AI strategy and capabilities.
